sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 6:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 6:1
items.html: The distance between the two ends of a degree, from the most refined to the coarsest, is called “length,” since so is the imaginary corporeal length, implying the area between it top end and its bottom end.
